artifacts.summary: 3-phase implementation plan: move 2 generic files to Foundations, replace 32 redundant theorems with bridge wrappers, verify build

memory_candidates.content: When bridging fc-polymorphic concrete DerivationTree theorems to generic Foundations equivalents via wrap/unwrap, input-taking theorems cannot directly wrap their fc-level inputs to Base-level Nonempty terms (since Base <= fc means we can lift FROM Base TO fc, not reverse). The correct pattern is: derive the curried generic form at Base, lift to fc, then apply modus ponens with the concrete fc-level inputs. No-input theorems use simple unwrap + lift.
